nodes.excerpt:
We’re pleased to announce that the Kotlin Foundation Grant Program is back for 2026. The program provides financial support to developers who create and maintain open-source libraries, tools, and frameworks that strengthen the Kotlin ecosystem. We continue to welcome projects related to Kotlin Multiplatform, as well as libraries focused on AI and large language models.

As in previous years, grant recipients retain ownership of their work. The Kotlin Foundation does not claim any intellectual property rights or trademarks from supported projects.

To be eligible, projects should meet the following criteria:

Open-source license. The project should use a permissive open-source license and be publicly available. Active maintenance. The project should demonstrate ongoing development and community engagement. Impact on the Kotlin ecosystem. The project should address a meaningful need for Kotlin developers and show a clear path for future improvements.

Submit your grant proposals before July 14, 2026. Applications will be reviewed by the Kotlin Foundation’s Ecosystem Committee based on impact, project quality, long-term sustainability, and alignment with Kotlin development best practices.

If you have any questions, reach out to us at grants@kotlinfoundation.org or join the discussion in the #kotlin-foundation channel on Kotlin Slack.

To see the projects selected in previous years, take a look at our earlier winner announcements. In 2025, the program received 117 applications and recognized projects including Firebase Kotlin SDK, Shadow Gradle Plugin, Mokkery, Stack Trace Decoroutinator, and Kobweb.

frontmatter.title: Apply for the 2026 Kotlin Foundation Grant Program for Library Authors

nodes.excerpt:
The Kotlin Foundation is pleased to announce the recipient of the KMP Contest Starter Kit Grant: Mirzamehdi Karimov. The Kotlin Foundation team responsible for evaluating the submissions was genuinely impressed by the thoughtfulness of the submissions and shares a heartfelt thank you to everybody who took the time to share their ideas of what a compelling KMP Contest Starter Kit would look like.

As far as next steps, the Kotlin Foundation will work with the grant recipient over the course of the next 2+ months to get the starter kit repo and associated content published. It will be available for participants of the 2027 KMP Contest.

Read more about the grant: Create This Year’s KMP Contest Starter Kit.

frontmatter.title: KMP Contest Starter Kit 2026 Grant Recipient
